resolution_note: OpenAlex works?filter=cites: endpoint returned HTTP 402/'Insufficient budget' (rate-limited, resets at UTC midnight) in this environment; single-entity OpenAlex lookups worked. Fell back to Semantic Scholar Graph API (api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/DOI:.../citations) as instructed for no-DOI cases, adapted here because the primary citation-list call failed. Semantic Scholar reports citationCount=10, matching the 10 records returned by the citations endpoint, so this is treated as the complete citing-works list.

citing_works.title: Systematic comparison of color representations between humans and deep neural networks: towards predicting human color perception in a vast color space
citing_works.method: Gromov-Wasserstein Optimal Transport (GWOT) alignment of DNN embeddings to human color similarity structure

citing_works.title: Measuring the Diversity of Qualia: Category-Theoretic Indices for Psychophysical Experimental Data
citing_works.task: subtyping (quantifying individual/group diversity of qualia structures)
citing_works.method: category-theoretic diversity indices (generalized magnitude, spread) applied to dissimilarity matrices

citing_works.title: Common Phenomenal and Neural Substrate Geometry in Visual Motion Perception
citing_works.task: conceptual/methodological citation (qualia-structure paradigm extended to visual motion, not color)
citing_works.method: n/a - cites approach, not this dataset's data

citing_works.title: A multi-level visual representation dataset for large-scale non-financial information disclosure
citing_works.task: unrelated domain (corporate sustainability report visual analysis); appears to cite as a Scientific Data data-descriptor precedent

citing_works.title: Robust individual alignment of color qualia structures: toward a structure-based taxonomy of divergent color experiences
citing_works.task: subtyping (individual-to-individual color qualia structure alignment / taxonomy of color-neurotypical vs atypical clusters)
citing_works.method: unsupervised GWOT alignment on new 11-subject pairwise similarity ratings
citing_works.uses_dataset_directly: extends the same paradigm with new data; same lab

citing_works.title: Linking the structure of neuronal mechanisms to the structure of qualia
citing_works.task: theoretical review (survey of structural approaches to qualia, cites the dataset/paradigm as an example)

citing_works.title: Qualia structures collapse for geometric shapes, but not faces, when spatial attention is withdrawn
citing_works.task: biomarker/subtyping (attentional modulation of qualia-structure alignment accuracy)
citing_works.method: similarity-rating dual-task paradigm + GWOT alignment (same lab's paradigm, different stimulus sets)

citing_works.title: On a confusion about there being two types of consciousness
citing_works.task: theoretical/conceptual citation (phenomenal vs access consciousness debate)

citing_works.title: A large-scale heterogeneous 3D magnetic resonance brain imaging dataset for self-supervised learning (FOMO260K)
citing_works.task: foundation-model (raw MRI aggregated as one of 910 public sources for SSL pretraining corpus)
citing_works.method: self-supervised pretraining corpus construction; not a qualia/consciousness analysis
citing_works.uses_dataset_directly: yes, but only the raw MRI volumes, not the color-qualia task/behavioral data

citing_works.title: An OpenMind for 3D Medical Vision Self-supervised Learning
citing_works.task: foundation-model (114k brain MRI volumes aggregated for SSL pretraining/benchmarking)
citing_works.method: self-supervised pretraining corpus construction and benchmarking (CNN/Transformer)
citing_works.uses_dataset_directly: yes, but only the raw MRI volumes, not the color-qualia task/behavioral data

dominant_tasks: foundation-model pretraining corpus reuse (raw MRI aggregated into large-scale SSL datasets, unrelated to the color-qualia task itself)
color-qualia structural analysis / subtyping (individual-differences alignment, diversity quantification, attention effects) — mostly same-lab (Tsuchiya/Oizumi group) follow-ups
theoretical/conceptual citation (consciousness-structure reviews and opinion pieces, no direct data reuse)
saturation_rationale: Only 10 citing works ~16 months post-publication. Of these, just 2-4 works actually reanalyze or extend the specific color-qualia similarity-judgment paradigm/data, and those are overwhelmingly from the originating lab (Tsuchiya, Oizumi and close collaborators) rather than independent external groups. Two additional citations reuse only the raw MRI volumes as anonymous pretraining material for unrelated self-supervised foundation-model corpora (FOMO260K, OpenMind), not the behavioral/report-vs-no-report qualia design. The remaining citations are theoretical/review pieces that cite the paradigm conceptually without touching the data. No external-validation, segmentation, outcome-prediction, or causal-inference use has yet appeared, and no independent group outside the original team has reanalyzed the with/without-report neuroimaging contrast. This indicates the dataset is still in an early, largely single-group exploitation phase with substantial white space for independent secondary analyses (e.g., decoding of qualia structure from the fMRI data itself, report-vs-no-report neural correlate analyses, or cross-lab replication).
